title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Financial Investment
Financial needs change steadily across different stages of life. Every life stage comes with its own financial priorities. Early adulthood often focuses on growth opportunities and wealth creation, mid-life involves balancing multiple responsibilities, and later years emphasise financial stability. These transitions call for a consistent yet adaptable financial investment approach that can support you through different stages of life.
Money can grow with some effort if investments are chosen wisely to match different stages of life. By using the right financial planning tips and structuring investments to match different stages of life, individuals can allow their money to work quietly in the background while they focus on careers, family, and future plans.
What is a Financial Investment?
A financial investment is the process of allocating funds to financial instruments such as equities, bonds, or savings-based products with the objective of generating returns. Each investment is defined by three key factors: risk level, potential return, and investment term, which together determine its suitability for different financial goals.
Financial investments may range from low-risk savings options like fixed deposits to higher-risk, growth-focused avenues such as private equity that support diversification. Since all investments involve inherent risk, including capital loss, evaluating risk tolerance and financial objectives is necessary before making any kind of investment decision.
How Do Financial Investments Work?
Financial investments work on the principle of allowing money to grow rather than remain idle. It involves allocating money to different assets with the expectation that they will grow in value or generate income over time in the form of interest or dividends. The nature of returns generated depends on the type of investment and how long the money remains invested.
The time period of a financial investment can vary widely based on the goal it is meant to serve. Some investments are chosen to support short-term goals, such as gathering funds over the next 5 years to buy a car, while others are planned with an aim to achieve long-term goals like retirement planning over several decades. Aligning the investment duration with the intended goal is essential for achieving your predefined goals within the set deadlines.
Importance of Financial Investment
The importance of financial investment lies in maintaining control during both stable and uncertain periods. Planned investments help manage risks, cover emergencies, and protect long-term objectives without putting excessive financial strain.
At the same time, financial investment enables gradual wealth creation and helps achieve long-term goals.
Types of Financial Investments
Financial investment types can be grouped based on their purpose, risk level, and return potential. Each type serves a different financial objective, whether the focus is on growth, stability, income generation, or long-term protection. Understanding these categories helps individuals choose investments that align with their financial goals and risk tolerance before making decisions.
Annuities
Annuities are retirement-oriented life insurance plans that can help you create a retirement corpus. Moreover, with immediate annuity plans, you can secure a regular and lifelong income before or after retirement. Deferred annuity plans are available as traditional savings plans that can help in creating a stable retirement corpus, which give you the benefit of insurance protection and market-linked returns. You can assess the type of annuity that would fulfil your needs and buy a suitable plan.
Best Time to Invest
The best time to buy annuity plans depends on your retirement planning needs. You can buy deferred annuity plans at a younger age, when you are working, so that you can create your retirement corpus over the long term. Immediate annuity plans are suitable when you have accumulated a corpus and want to secure regular annuity income from it.
How to Purchase?
You can buy annuity plans from life insurance companies online or offline. For the online mode, you can visit the insurer’s website, assess the plans available, choose the suitable one and buy it by filling out the online proposal form. For offline plans, you can visit the nearest branch office of the insurer and buy the policy.
Bonds
A bond is a type of investment avenue that can offer fixed interest income. When you buy bonds, you essentially lend money to a government or company, and the bond issuer offers regular interest payments and repayment of the principal at maturity. There are three main kinds of bond viz, government, municipal, and corporate bonds.
Best Time to Invest
The right time to invest in bonds depends on your financial goals and investment strategy. You can buy the bond anytime and create a corpus for your short, medium or long-term financial goals.
How to Purchase?
You can buy government bonds through banks or post offices, while corporate bonds and debentures are available via brokers and financial institutions. You can also buy bonds online. Most platforms allow investors to complete KYC digitally and choose to compare a wide variety of bonds to invest in
Certificates of Deposit
A Certificate of Deposit (CD) is a fixed-income instrument issued by banks and financial institutions that offers assured returns in the form of predefined interest rates for a fixed tenure. The invested amount is locked in until maturity, making CDs a relatively safe short-term investment option.
Best Time to Invest
The best time to buy CDs again depends on your requirements. Since CDs have a short-term investment horizon, you can buy them to create a corpus for your short-term goals.
How to Purchase?
CDs are available through banks as well as brokers who offer brokered certificates of deposit. Since deposit brokers are not regulated or licensed, it is essential to review their background and confirm the reliability of the issuing institution before investing.
Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)
An Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) is a fund that tracks a market index such as the Sensex or Nifty. ETFs trade on stock exchanges like shares, with prices changing during market hours based on the value of the underlying assets.
Best Time to Invest
There is no specific time to invest in ETFs. You can invest in them anytime you want. You can use ETFs to plan for your short, medium, or long-term goals since they are liquid and can be redeemed anytime. That being said, if you stay invested, you can get the benefit of compounded returns, which can help in enhancing your savings.
How to Purchase?
ETFs are bought and sold on stock exchanges in the same way as equity shares. Investors place buy or sell orders through a Demat and trading account, specifying the number of units and the price.
High-Yield Savings Accounts
A high-yield savings account is a savings account that offers a higher annual percentage yield than traditional savings options. Interest is calculated and compounded at specified intervals.
Best Time to Invest
You can open a savings account anytime to park your funds and meet your basic banking needs. It is suitable to open a savings account as early as possible for hassle-free deposits, withdrawals and other banking services. Moreover, you can use the savings account to set aside an emergency fund.
How to Purchase?
A high-yield savings account is mostly offered by financial institutions and online banks. To open a high-yield savings account, choose a bank or financial institution offering competitive APYs. Complete the application online or in person, provide your personal and identity details, and fund the account through a linked bank transfer or deposit. Once the account is active, you start earning interest.
Money Market Funds
A money market fund is a type of debt mutual fund that invests its portfolio primarily in short-term debt instruments, cash, and other forms of cash equivalents. They focus on liquidity and capital preservation, offering lower risk but comparatively lower returns.
Best Time to Invest
You can invest in a money market fund anytime you want to invest in debt instruments with a short-term horizon.
How to Purchase?
Money market funds can be purchased directly from the fund house or through authorised brokers and online investment platforms. Investors buy and redeem units with the fund itself, not on a stock exchange, and can usually sell their holdings on any business day at the prevailing net asset value (NAV).
Mutual Funds
A mutual fund is an investment vehicle that pools money from multiple investors and invests it in assets such as equities, bonds, and money market instruments. The investments are managed by professional fund managers, and returns are shared among investors based on the fund’s net asset value (NAV), after deducting applicable expenses.
Best Time to Invest
You can invest in a mutual fund anytime to build a corpus for different financial goals. That being said, it is better to invest from an early age so that you can give your mutual fund investments time to grow with compounding returns.
How to Purchase?
Investors can choose between a regular and a direct mutual fund. Regular plans involve intermediaries who assist with investing, while direct plans allow investors to invest on their own at a lower cost, which can improve returns over time.
Retirement Plans
Retirement plans are savings plans that can help you create a corpus for retirement and also secure a regular income in older ages. There are different types of retirement plans available in the market, like life insurance annuity plans, National Pension System (NPS), Atal Pension Yojana (APY), etc.
Best Time to Invest
If you are planning for retirement, it is better to start investing early. This would help you save affordably and build up a good corpus over time through the benefit of compounded returns.
How to Purchase?
The mode of purchasing retirement plans depends on the type of plan that you choose. For instance, life insurance annuity plans can be bought from life insurers, while you can open an NPS account through your bank. Both online and offline modes of investments are available for most retirement plans.
Stocks
A stock represents a small ownership stake in a company, and each unit of ownership is called a share. By owning shares, investors can benefit from a company’s profits by earning dividends.
Best Time to Invest
The right time to buy stocks depends on your investment strategy. You can start investing in stocks as soon as you start earning and plan a financial portfolio. Alternatively, you can wait out some years, learn the ropes of investing and then start investing in stocks. So, the right time depends on your preference and financial needs.
How to Purchase?
To buy shares, you need a Demat and trading account. After depositing money in your demat account, choose the stock and place a buy order. Once the trade is completed, the shares appear in your Demat account.
How to Start Investing?
Beginning an investment journey can feel uncertain, especially for first-time investors. A structured approach helps build confidence and provides clarity on how to move forward in a disciplined way.
Learn from the Right Experts
With numerous investment options available, selecting the right ones can be confusing.  Financial experts assess your goals, time horizon, and financial responsibilities to suggest options that fit your specific requirements.
Understand Your Risk Tolerance
Every investment carries a certain level of risk, and the possibility of higher returns usually comes with greater risk. Understanding your risk tolerance level can help you select investment options that match your comfort level and stay invested during market ups and downs.
Start Small
There is no fixed amount required to start investing; it depends on your income, financial commitments, and long-term goals. Starting with a small amount and gradually increasing it over time is often more effective than waiting for the right time to invest.
Learn More About Taxes
Section 80C of the Income Tax Act allows tax deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh per financial year on eligible investments. ELSS stands out among these options due to its higher return potential and the shortest lock-in period of three years.
Investing and Spreading Your Risk
Spreading your investments across different asset classes, sectors, and instruments helps reduce overall risk. Diversification ensures that poor performance in one investment does not significantly impact your entire portfolio, allowing for more stable and balanced long-term returns.
Key Takeaways
Your financial needs change as life moves forward, so your investments should adapt with you, supporting growth early on and stability later in life.
Investing allows your money to work for you over time, helping it grow or generate income instead of sitting idle.
Every investment comes with some level of risk, which makes it important to choose options that match your goals and comfort level.
A thoughtful investment approach helps build financial security, independence, and long-term wealth while supporting important life goals.
Spreading investments across different options reduces risk and protects you from relying too heavily on a single investment.
Starting small, staying consistent, and learning along the way can make investing manageable and rewarding over the long run.
Conclusion
Financial investment is not about chasing quick returns or following trends. It is about building a structured approach that grows with you, adapts to life’s changes, and supports both present needs and future aspirations. A well-planned financial investment strategy balances both growth and capital protection.
Life and health insurance adds an extra layer of protection to this plan. It helps safeguard your future goals during unexpected situations by giving families financial stability and allowing long-term investments to stay on track even during difficult times.
FAQs
What is the best financial investment?
The best financial investment depends entirely on your risk appetite, investment horizon, financial planning, and investment strategy. For some investors, mutual funds can be the best financial investment, while others might favour fixed deposits.
What is financial investment risk?
Financial investment risk is the chance that an investment may result in a loss or lower-than-expected returns due to uncertainty.
Why invest in financial investments?
Investing in financial investments helps support financial security, grow wealth over time, and achieve life goals. It also supports long-term independence by preparing you for future needs and unexpected expenses.
Which investment is best in future?
The choice of the best investment is not universal. It depends on your risk profile, investment preference, investment tenure, financial goals and other factors. So, assess your needs and preferences and then choose investments that match them.
What are examples of financial investments?
Examples of financial investments include stocks, bonds and debentures, mutual funds, exchange-traded funds (ETFs), fixed deposits, certificates of deposit (CDs), and insurance–based investment plans.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Financial Investment
Financial needs change steadily across different stages of life. Every life stage comes with its own financial priorities. Early adulthood often focuses on growth opportunities and wealth creation, mid- life involves balancing multiple responsibilities, and later years emphasise financial stability. These transitions call for a consistent yet adaptable financial investment approach that can support you through different stages of life.
Money can grow with some effort if investments are chosen wisely to match different stages of life. By using the right financial planning tips and structuring investments to match different stages of life, individuals can allow their money to work quietly in the background while they focus on careers, family, and future plans.
What is a Financial Investment?
A financial investment is the process of allocating funds to financial instruments such as equities, bonds, or savings-based products with the objective of generating returns. Each investment is defined by three key factors: risk level, potential return, and investment term, which together determine its suitability for different financial goals.
Financial investments may range from low-risk savings options like fixed deposits to higher-risk, growth-focused avenues such as equity or other market-linked options. Since all investments involve inherent risk, including capital loss, evaluating risk tolerance and financial objectives is necessary before making any kind of investment decision.
How Do Financial Investments Work?
Financial investments work on the principle of allowing money to grow rather than remain idle. It involves allocating money to different assets with the expectation that they will grow in value or generate income over time in the form of interest or dividends. The nature of returns generated depends on the type of investment and how long the money remains invested.
The time period of a financial investment can vary widely based on the goal it is meant to serve. Some investments are chosen to support short-term goals, such as gathering funds over the next 5 years to buy a car, while others are planned with an aim to achieve long-term goals like retirement planning over several decades. Aligning the investment duration with the intended goal is essential for achieving your predefined goals within the set deadlines.
Importance of Financial Investment
The importance of financial investment lies in maintaining control during both stable and uncertain periods. Planned investments help manage risks, cover emergencies, and protect long-term objectives without putting excessive financial strain.
At the same time, financial investment enables gradual wealth creation and helps achieve long-term goals.
Types of Financial Investments
Financial investment types can be grouped based on their purpose, risk level, and return potential. Each type serves a different financial objective, whether the focus is on growth, stability, income generation, or long-term financial protection. Understanding these categories helps individuals choose investments that align with their financial goals and risk tolerance before making decisions. Below mentioned are some types of financial investment options -
1. Annuities
Annuities are retirement-oriented plans that can help you create a retirement corpus. Moreover, with immediate annuity plans, you can secure a regular and lifelong income before or after retirement. Deferred annuity plans are available as savings plans that can help in creating a stable retirement corpus. There are ULIP-oriented deferred annuity plans too which give you the benefit of life insurance protection and market-linked returns. You can assess the type of annuity that would fulfil your financial needs and buy a suitable plan.
Best Time to Invest
The best time to buy annuity plans depends on your retirement planning needs. You can buy deferred annuity plans at a younger age, when you are working, so that you can create your retirement corpus over the long term. Immediate annuity plans are suitable when you have accumulated a corpus and want to secure regular annuity income from it.
How to Purchase?
You can buy annuity plans from life insurance companies online or offline. For the online mode, you can visit the insurer’s website, assess the plans available, choose the suitable one and buy it by filling out the online proposal form. For offline plans, you can visit the nearest branch office of the insurer and buy the policy.
2. Bonds
A bond is a type of investment avenue that can offer fixed interest income. When you buy bonds, you essentially lend money to a government or company, and the bond issuer offers regular interest payments and repayment of the principal at maturity. There are different types of bonds and you can choose those that align with your financial needs.
Best Time to Invest
The right time to invest in bonds depends on your financial goals and investment strategy. You can buy the bond anytime and create a corpus for your short, medium or long-term financial goals.
How to Purchase?
You can buy government bonds through banks or post offices, while corporate bonds and debentures are available via brokers and financial institutions. You can also buy bonds
online. Most platforms allow investors to complete KYC digitally and choose to compare a wide variety of bonds to invest in
3. Certificates of Deposit1
A Certificate of Deposit (CD) is a fixed-income instrument issued by banks and financial institutions that offers assured returns in the form of predefined interest rates for a fixed tenure. The invested amount is locked in until maturity and if you withdraw prematurely, there’s a penalty..
Best Time to Invest
The best time to buy CDs again depends on your requirements. Since CDs have a short-term investment horizon, you can use them to create a corpus for your short-term goals.
How to Purchase?
CD is a financial instrument with fixed-income facilities and assured payout. They can be purchased and and sold like shares. A buyer and seller agree on the quality and price of the CD transaction. Once agreed, the seller issues a delivery instruction slip that allows the depository participant to transfer the CD to the buyer’s account by debiting the seller’s account.
4. Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)
An Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) is a fund that track market indices such as Sensex or Nifty. ETFs trade on stock exchanges like shares, with prices changing during market hours based on the value of the underlying assets.
Best Time to Invest
There is no specific time to invest in ETFs. You can invest in them anytime you want. You can use ETFs to plan for your short, medium, or long-term goals since they are liquid and can be redeemed anytime. That being said, if you stay invested, you can get the benefit of compounded returns, which can help in enhancing your savings.
How to Purchase?
ETFs are bought and sold on stock exchanges in the same way as equity shares. Investors place buy or sell orders through a Demat and trading account, specifying the number of units and the price.
5. High-Yield Savings Accounts
A high-yield savings account is a savings account that offers a high annual yield.
Best Time to Invest
You can open a savings account anytime to park your funds and meet your basic banking needs. It is suitable to open a savings account as early as possible for hassle-free deposits, withdrawals and other banking services. Moreover, you can use the savings account to set aside an emergency fund.
How to Purchase?
You can choose a bank offering a high-yield savings account. Apply with the bank to open the account by filling out an application form and submitting the required documents.
6. Money Market Funds
A money market fund is a type of debt mutual fund that invests its portfolio primarily in short-term debt instruments, cash, and other forms of cash equivalents. They focus on liquidity and capital preservation, offering low risk but comparatively lower returns.
Best Time to Invest
You can invest in a money market fund anytime you want to invest in debt instruments with a short-term horizon.
How to Purchase?
Money market funds can be purchased directly from the fund house or through authorised brokers and online investment platforms..
7. Mutual Funds
A mutual fund is an investment vehicle that pools money from multiple investors and invests it in assets such as equities, bonds, and money market instruments. The investments are managed by professional fund managers, and returns are shared among investors based on the fund’s net asset value (NAV), after deducting applicable expenses.
Best Time to Invest
You can invest in a mutual fund anytime to build a corpus for different financial goals. That being said, starting early is recommended so that you can give your mutual fund investments time to grow with compounding returns.
How to Purchase?
You can invest in mutual funds directly with the fund house or through authorised brokers. Choose a scheme that you like and invest offline or online in some simple steps. .
8. Retirement Plans
Retirement plans are savings plans that can help you create a corpus for retirement and also secure a regular income in older ages. There are different types of retirement plans available in the market, like National Pension System (NPS), Atal Pension Yojana (APY), etc.
Best Time to Invest
If you are planning for retirement, it is better to start investing early. This would help you save affordably and build up a corpus over time through the benefit of compounded returns.
How to Purchase?
The mode of purchasing retirement plans depends on the type of plan that you choose. For instance, annuity plans can be bought from life insurers, while you can open an NPS account through your bank. Both online and offline modes of investments are available for most retirement plans.
9. Stocks
A stock represents a small ownership stake in a company, and each unit of ownership is called a share. By owning shares, investors can benefit from a company’s profits by earning dividends.
Best Time to Invest
The right time to buy stocks depends on your investment strategy. You may start investing in stocks as soon as you start earning and plan a financial portfolio. Alternatively, you can wait out some years, learn the ropes of investing and then start. So, the right time depends on your preference and financial needs.
How to Purchase?
To buy shares, you need a Demat and trading account. After depositing money in your demat account, choose the stock and place a buy order. Once the trade is completed, the shares appear in your Demat account.
How to Start Investing?
Beginning an investment journey can feel uncertain, especially for first-time investors. A structured approach helps build confidence and provides clarity on how to move forward in a disciplined way.
1. Learn from the Right Experts
With numerous investment options available, selecting the right ones can be confusing. Financial experts assess your goals, time horizon, and financial responsibilities to suggest options that fit your specific requirements.
2. Understand Your Risk Tolerance
Every investment carries a certain level of risk, and the possibility of higher returns usually comes with greater risk. Understanding your risk tolerance level can help you select investment options that match your comfort level and stay invested during market ups and downs.
3. Start Small
There is no fixed amount required to start investing; it depends on your income, financial commitments, and long-term goals. Starting with a small amount and gradually increasing it over time is often more effective than waiting for the right time to invest.
4. Learn More About Taxes
Learn about the tax deductions available with various investment options to minimize tax liability. For instance - Section 80C of the Income Tax Act,1961 allows tax deductions of up to ₹1.5 lakh per financial year on eligible investments.
5. Investing and Spreading Your Risk
Spreading your investments across different asset classes, sectors, and instruments helps reduce overall risk. Diversification ensures that poor performance in one investment does not significantly impact your entire portfolio, allowing for more stable and balanced long- term returns.
Key Takeaways
1. Your financial needs change as life moves forward, so your investments should adapt with
you, supporting growth early on and stability later in life. 2. Investing allows your money to work for you over time, helping it grow or generate income
instead of sitting idle. 3. Every investment comes with some level of risk, which makes it important to choose options
that match your goals and comfort level. 4. A thoughtful investment approach helps build financial security, independence, and long-
term wealth while supporting important life goals. 5. Spreading investments across different options reduces risk and protects you from relying
too heavily on a single investment. 6. Starting small, staying consistent, and learning along the way can make investing
manageable and rewarding over the long run.
Conclusion
Financial investment is not about chasing quick returns or following trends. It is about building a structured approach that grows with you, adapts to life’s changes, and supports both present needs and future aspirations. A well-planned financial investment strategy balances both growth and capital protection.
Life and health insurance adds an extra layer of financial protection. It helps safeguard your future goals during unexpected situations by giving families financial stability and allowing long-term investments to stay on track even during difficult times.
FAQs
1. What is the best financial investment?
The best financial investment depends entirely on your risk appetite, investment horizon, financial planning, and investment strategy. For some investors, mutual funds can be the best financial investment, while others might favour fixed deposits.
2. What is financial investment risk?
Financial investment risk is the chance that an investment may result in a loss or lower-than- expected returns due to uncertainty.
3. Why invest in financial investments?
Investing in financial investments helps support financial security, grow wealth over time, and achieve life goals. It also supports long-term independence by preparing you for future needs and unexpected expenses.
.
4. What are examples of financial investments?
Examples of financial investments include stocks, bonds and debentures, mutual funds, exchange-traded funds (ETFs), fixed deposits, certificates of deposit (CDs), etc..

changes.word_original: The invested amount is locked in until maturity, making CDs a relatively safe short-term investment option.
changes.pdf_final: The invested amount is locked in until maturity and if you withdraw prematurely, there’s a penalty..

changes.word_original: Since CDs have a short-term investment horizon, you can buy them to create a corpus for your short-term goals.
changes.pdf_final: Since CDs have a short-term investment horizon, you can use them to create a corpus for your short-term goals.

changes.word_original: CDs are available through banks as well as brokers who offer brokered certificates of deposit.
changes.pdf_final: CD is a financial instrument with fixed-income facilities and assured payout.

changes.word_original: Since deposit brokers are not regulated or licensed, it is essential to review their background and confirm the reliability of the issuing institution before investing.
changes.pdf_final: They can be purchased and and sold like shares.
A buyer and seller agree on the quality and price of the CD transaction.
Once agreed, the seller issues a delivery instruction slip that allows the depository participant to transfer the CD to the buyer’s account by debiting the seller’s account.

changes.word_original: An Exchange Traded Fund (ETF) is a fund that tracks a market index such as the Sensex or Nifty.

changes.word_original: To open a high-yield savings account, choose a bank or financial institution offering competitive APYs.

changes.word_original: Complete the application online or in person, provide your personal and identity details, and fund the account through a linked bank transfer or deposit.

changes.word_original: Investors buy and redeem units with the fund itself, not on a stock exchange, and can usually sell their holdings on any business day at the prevailing net asset value (NAV).

changes.word_original: Which investment is best in future?
The choice of the best investment is not universal.
It depends on your risk profile, investment preference, investment tenure, financial goals and other factors.

changes.word_original: Disclaimer
changes.pdf_final: Disclaimer IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract.
The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors.

The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions.
Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company.
The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
